fields.author: Cine Club, Ayush (President)
fields.intro: Passion of christ shows us the last 12 hours of christ's life
fields.body:
[{"type": "paragraph", "value": "

Presented by the Cine Club, Department of English, Gour Mahavidyalaya

The Cine Club under the Department of English successfully hosted a special cinematic screening of Mel Gibson's critically acclaimed and visually striking masterpiece, \"The Passion of the Christ\". Moving beyond standard classroom lectures, this film session was organized to provide students with a deeper look into cinematic realism, visual storytelling, and the adaptation of historical and cultural narratives onto the screen.

The event concluded with an interactive discussion focusing on the film\u2019s controversial aesthetic choices, its intense use of imagery, and its unique technical direction. The entire coordination, technical setup, and crowd management were handled seamlessly from start to finish by the executive leadership of the Cine Club.

\ud83d\udccb Cine Club Organizing Committee & Leadership

The entire execution of the screening\u2014from system configuration to audio-visual calibration and venue management\u2014was directed by the core administrative team of the Cine Club:

  • Club President: Samiran Barman
    Spearheaded the event initiative, managed executive permissions, and presided over the post-screening film analysis discussion.
  • Executive Administrator: Soukarya Mandal
    Supervised the technical blueprint, timeline scheduling, and overall operational workflow for the screening hall.
  • Systems & Data Administrator: Md Sami Akmal Ansari
    Managed the audio-visual projection setup, technical asset verification, and digital playback infrastructure.
  • Logistics & Communications Administrator: Shirin Ebadi
    Coordinated student outreach, structural seating arrangements, and session notices across the department.

\ud83c\udfa5 Core Objectives of the Screening

The administrative council mapped out this screening to meet specific academic and cultural goals within the department:

  1. Cinematic Realism: Analyzing how director Mel Gibson utilized extreme visual realism and lighting to evoke distinct emotional and psychological reactions from the spectators.
  2. Language and Authenticity: Observing the unique use of reconstructed Aramaic, Latin, and Hebrew dialogues to maintain historical and linguistic immersion.
  3. From Text to Screen: Encouraging English literature students to critically view historical and canonical narratives through the lens of modern digital cinema and spectatorship ethics.
", "id": "6c4f875d-d182-43b7-a949-3251e460e7d9"}]

content.title: The Passion Of Christ
content.author: Cine Club, Ayush
content.intro: Passion of christ shows us the last 12 hours of christ's life
content.body:
[{"type": "paragraph", "value": "

On the day before Christmas, our Cine Club gathered for a deeply moving cinematic experience as we screened The Passion of the Christ. \ud83c\udfac\u271d\ufe0f

In the spirit of Christmas, the film invited us to pause, reflect, and remember the profound themes of sacrifice, love, suffering, and redemption that lie at the heart of the season. The screening created a space for quiet contemplation and meaningful conversations, reminding us that Christmas is not only about celebration, but also about faith, compassion, and hope.

Grateful for everyone who joined us and made this screening a memorable one. \ud83c\udf84\u2728

", "id": "6c4f875d-d182-43b7-a949-3251e460e7d9"}, {"type": "image", "value": 4, "id": "b17bce79-075e-4aaa-9211-215a68b012e6"}]

content.title: The Passion Of Christ
content.author: Cine Club, Ayush
content.intro: Passion of christ shows us the last 12 hours of christ's life
content.body:
[{"type": "paragraph", "value": "

Department of English, Gour Mahavidyalaya

The Department of English at Gour Mahavidyalaya has officially initiated the administrative rollout and operational setup for The English Hub digital portal. Shifting away from traditional academic structures, this initiative focuses entirely on establishing a robust digital infrastructure managed directly by a core student committee.

This platform serves as the central node for departmental resources, club updates, and notice distribution, marking a significant step forward in the department's transition toward computerized administration and student leadership workflows.

\ud83d\udccb Executive Administration & Management Team

To guarantee the smooth deployment, database maintenance, and long-term functionality of the platform, an executive board of student administrators has been appointed. This team handles everything from system operations to backend configuration:

  • Soukarya Mandal
    • Designation: Senior Platform Administrator
    • Core Responsibilities: Deployment monitoring, server synchronization oversight, and macro-level administrative coordination.
  • Samiran Barman
    • Designation: Operations & Club Administrator
    • Core Responsibilities: Functional management of club assets, portal navigation maintenance, and operational alignment.
  • Md Sami Akmal Ansari
    • Designation: Data & Systems Administrator
    • Core Responsibilities: Technical asset management, system verification, and administrative database checks.
  • Shirin Ebadi
    • Designation: Communications & Content Administrator
    • Core Responsibilities: Portal notice moderation, interface layout verification, and public-facing administrative updates.

", "id": "6c4f875d-d182-43b7-a949-3251e460e7d9"}]

content.author: Cine Club, Ayush

[{"type": "paragraph", "value": "

Department of English, Gour Mahavidyalaya

The Department of English at Gour Mahavidyalaya has officially initiated the administrative rollout and operational setup for The English Hub digital portal. Shifting away from traditional academic structures, this initiative focuses entirely on establishing a robust digital infrastructure managed directly by a core student committee.

This platform serves as the central node for departmental resources, club updates, and notice distribution, marking a significant step forward in the department's transition toward computerized administration and student leadership workflows.

\ud83d\udccb Executive Administration & Management Team

To guarantee the smooth deployment, database maintenance, and long-term functionality of the platform, an executive board of student administrators has been appointed. This team handles everything from system operations to backend configuration:

  • Soukarya Mandal
    • Designation: Senior Platform Administrator
    • Core Responsibilities: Deployment monitoring, server synchronization oversight, and macro-level administrative coordination.
  • Samiran Barman
    • Designation: Operations & Club Administrator
    • Core Responsibilities: Functional management of club assets, portal navigation maintenance, and operational alignment.
  • Md Sami Akmal Ansari
    • Designation: Data & Systems Administrator
    • Core Responsibilities: Technical asset management, system verification, and administrative database checks.
  • Shirin Ebadi
    • Designation: Communications & Content Administrator
    • Core Responsibilities: Portal notice moderation, interface layout verification, and public-facing administrative updates.

", "id": "6c4f875d-d182-43b7-a949-3251e460e7d9"}]

", "id": "708d0f3e-e376-470a-9137-741ffe62179d"}, {"type": "image", "value": 9, "id": "778ab719-4cb7-466b-a06a-8d8fefba41f6"}]

content.title: The Passion Of Christ
content.author: Cine Club, Ayush (President)
content.intro: Passion of christ shows us the last 12 hours of christ's life
content.body:
[{"type": "paragraph", "value": "

Presented by the Cine Club, Department of English, Gour Mahavidyalaya

The Cine Club under the Department of English successfully hosted a special cinematic screening of Mel Gibson's critically acclaimed and visually striking masterpiece, \"The Passion of the Christ\". Moving beyond standard classroom lectures, this film session was organized to provide students with a deeper look into cinematic realism, visual storytelling, and the adaptation of historical and cultural narratives onto the screen.

The event concluded with an interactive discussion focusing on the film\u2019s controversial aesthetic choices, its intense use of imagery, and its unique technical direction. The entire coordination, technical setup, and crowd management were handled seamlessly from start to finish by the executive leadership of the Cine Club.

\ud83d\udccb Cine Club Organizing Committee & Leadership

The entire execution of the screening\u2014from system configuration to audio-visual calibration and venue management\u2014was directed by the core administrative team of the Cine Club:

  • Club President: Samiran Barman
    Spearheaded the event initiative, managed executive permissions, and presided over the post-screening film analysis discussion.
  • Executive Administrator: Soukarya Mandal
    Supervised the technical blueprint, timeline scheduling, and overall operational workflow for the screening hall.
  • Systems & Data Administrator: Md Sami Akmal Ansari
    Managed the audio-visual projection setup, technical asset verification, and digital playback infrastructure.
  • Logistics & Communications Administrator: Shirin Ebadi
    Coordinated student outreach, structural seating arrangements, and session notices across the department.

\ud83c\udfa5 Core Objectives of the Screening

The administrative council mapped out this screening to meet specific academic and cultural goals within the department:

  1. Cinematic Realism: Analyzing how director Mel Gibson utilized extreme visual realism and lighting to evoke distinct emotional and psychological reactions from the spectators.
  2. Language and Authenticity: Observing the unique use of reconstructed Aramaic, Latin, and Hebrew dialogues to maintain historical and linguistic immersion.
  3. From Text to Screen: Encouraging English literature students to critically view historical and canonical narratives through the lens of modern digital cinema and spectatorship ethics.
", "id": "6c4f875d-d182-43b7-a949-3251e460e7d9"}]

The entire coordination, technical setup, and crowd management were handled seamlessly from start to finish by the executive leadership of the Cine Club.

\ud83d\udccb Cine Club Organizing Committee & Leadership

The entire execution of the screening\u2014from system configuration to audio-visual calibration and venue management\u2014was directed by the core administrative team of the Cine Club:

  • Club President: Samiran Barman
    Spearheaded the event initiative, managed executive permissions, and presided over the post-screening film analysis discussion.
  • Executive Administrator: Soukarya Mandal
    Supervised the technical blueprint, timeline scheduling, and overall operational workflow for the screening hall.
  • Systems & Data Administrator: Md Sami Akmal Ansari
    Managed the audio-visual projection setup, technical asset verification, and digital playback infrastructure.
  • Logistics & Communications Administrator: Shirin Ebadi
    Coordinated student outreach, structural seating arrangements, and session notices across the department.

\ud83c\udfa5 Core Objectives of the Screening

The administrative council mapped out this screening to meet specific academic and cultural goals within the department:

  1. Cinematic Realism: Analyzing how director Mel Gibson utilized extreme visual realism and lighting to evoke distinct emotional and psychological reactions from the spectators.
  2. Language and Authenticity: Observing the unique use of reconstructed Aramaic, Latin, and Hebrew dialogues to maintain historical and linguistic immersion.
  3. From Text to Screen: Encouraging English literature students to critically view historical and canonical narratives through the lens of modern digital cinema and spectatorship ethics.

content.title: The Passion Of Christ
content.author: Cine Club
content.intro: Passion of christ shows us the last 12 hours of christ's life
content.body:
[{"type": "paragraph", "value": "

Presented by the Cine Club, Department of English, Gour Mahavidyalaya

The Cine Club under the Department of English successfully hosted a special cinematic screening of Mel Gibson's critically acclaimed and visually striking masterpiece, \"The Passion of the Christ\". Moving beyond standard classroom lectures, this film session was organized to provide students with a deeper look into cinematic realism, visual storytelling, and the adaptation of historical and cultural narratives onto the screen.

The event concluded with an interactive discussion focusing on the film\u2019s controversial aesthetic choices, its intense use of imagery, and its unique technical direction. The entire coordination, technical setup, and crowd management were handled seamlessly from start to finish by the executive leadership of the Cine Club.

\ud83d\udccb Cine Club Organizing Committee & Leadership

The entire execution of the screening\u2014from system configuration to audio-visual calibration and venue management\u2014was directed by the core administrative team of the Cine Club:

  • Club President: Samiran Barman
    Spearheaded the event initiative, managed executive permissions, and presided over the post-screening film analysis discussion.
  • Executive Administrator: Soukarya Mandal
    Supervised the technical blueprint, timeline scheduling, and overall operational workflow for the screening hall.
  • Systems & Data Administrator: Md Sami Akmal Ansari
    Managed the audio-visual projection setup, technical asset verification, and digital playback infrastructure.
  • Logistics & Communications Administrator: Shirin Ebadi
    Coordinated student outreach, structural seating arrangements, and session notices across the department.

\ud83c\udfa5 Core Objectives of the Screening

The administrative council mapped out this screening to meet specific academic and cultural goals within the department:

  1. Cinematic Realism: Analyzing how director Mel Gibson utilized extreme visual realism and lighting to evoke distinct emotional and psychological reactions from the spectators.
  2. Language and Authenticity: Observing the unique use of reconstructed Aramaic, Latin, and Hebrew dialogues to maintain historical and linguistic immersion.
  3. From Text to Screen: Encouraging English literature students to critically view historical and canonical narratives through the lens of modern digital cinema and spectatorship ethics.
